design_philosophy.core_loop: Navigate 3D environments, eliminate enemy waves using diverse weaponry (melee and ranged), solve environmental puzzles to progress through levels
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Action-oriented combat with puzzle-gating; managing limited resources (ammo, health) against varied enemy types in explorable spaces
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Play as Lo Wang, a skilled enforcer weaponmaster turned reluctant hero, using Japanese-themed and high-tech weaponry to dismantle a megacorporation's supernatural schemes
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Heavy emphasis on fast-paced, humor-driven action (tongue-in-cheek tone matching Duke Nukem 3D style); ambitious technical features like room-over-room architecture, vehicle combat, and turrets were unusual for 1997 FPS titles; melee katana combat as primary weapon alongside guns differentiates from contemporaries

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Standard FPS difficulty settings inferred from Build engine conventions; no adaptive difficulty documented

progression.upgrade_system: Weapon arsenal expansion throughout campaign (katana, shurikens, Uzis, riot gun, railgun, sticky bombs, enemy heads/hearts as weapons); multiple firing modes unlock on certain weapons
progression.unlock_mechanism: Story-gated progression through level-based campaign; puzzles must be solved to access subsequent areas; new weapons discovered during progression
replayability_hooks: Multiple weapon options per encounter encourage different tactical approaches
Environmental exploration for secrets and alternate paths

world_building_techniques: Environmental storytelling through futuristic Japan setting controlled by Zilla Enterprises
Character-driven narrative (Lo Wang's mentor relationship, betrayal, personal vendetta against Master Zilla)
Dialogue and environmental cues establish megacorporate corruption and supernatural threat
Weapon pickup flavor text and Lo Wang's reactive one-liners (e.g., 'Time to get erased!' for railgun) reinforce tone and character

boss_design.philosophy: Climactic encounter emphasizes spectacle and character conflict; Master Zilla final battle involves piloting a massive samurai-styled war mech, combining vehicle combat with melee/ranged weaponry mastery
notable_bosses: Master Zilla (final encounter in samurai war mech)

danger_signals: Master Zilla's unchecked corporate dominance and corruption
Lo Wang's mentor assassination signals personal stakes and escalating threat
environmental_storytelling: Futuristic Japan controlled by single conglomerate suggests totalitarian corporate landscape
Mix of high-tech facilities and supernatural/mystical environments reflects corruption of nature by corporate power
Japanese aesthetic and themes (katana, dojo references, Eastern mythology) inform world flavor
